methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $7.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Stegra is a Growth-stage climate and energy company founded by Carl-Erik Lagercrantz and Harald Mix that has raised $7B+ to date. The valuation benchmark universe comprises 19 same-sector peers, including 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private firms such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, the narrow peer set—filtered by stage, valuation tier, and operational similarity—yielded insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3) to support a statistically robust peer-median multiple approach.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation technique here, using Stegra's $7.0B total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $8.4B. The analysis generates a triangulated range with a low of $7.1B, median of $8.4B, and high of $9.7B, anchored to this single method.

The valuation range of $7.1B–$9.7B (median $8.4B) is supportable by stage-typical progression dynamics but remains constrained by the absence of tight peer comparables. Key uncertainties include: (1) the representativeness of the 1.2x multiplier to Stegra's specific market and execution profile, and (2) the material gap between public and private valuations in climate-tech, which comps do not adequately bridge. Narrowing this range would require either additional same-stage peers or forward-looking revenue and margin data.
